beats.narration: A weld is a small casting made under terrible conditions. Every process solves those problems differently, and leaves fingerprints you can read on film.

payload.title: WELD FORENSICS
payload.sub: Know the process, predict the flaw.

beats.narration: Flux processes, stick and submerged arc, trap slag. Gas-shielded solid wire risks lack of fusion. Flux-cored wire brings slag back. Tungsten arc sheds fragments.
On film, trapped slag and unfused sidewalls image dark. Tungsten is denser than steel, so its fragments print as sharp light spots.

payload.heading: Dark or light tells you what it is

beats.narration: Light spot in a TIG root? Tungsten. Slag on solid-wire MIG? No flux. Be suspicious. Stuck spot weld? Invisible to radiography.

payload.heading: Match the call to the process

chips.t: Light spot in a TIG root

beats.narration: So before you read any film, ask what process made the weld. Process first, film second. That's interpreting instead of guessing.
